evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Ensure the accuracy, consistency and reliability of data across datasets, reports and dashboards.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour signals (accuracy, consistency, reliability, attention to detail) dominate with no velocity/speed language present.
evidence.domain_risk: Experience/knowledge in Billing, Revenue Assurance & Fraud systems will be an added advantage.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Billing/revenue assurance/fraud is mentioned only as an added advantage rather than a core requirement, and the JD's dominant framing is generic reporting/dashboard accuracy, placing it at moderate risk per the tie-breaker rule.
evidence.data_team_maturity: You'll own the transformation layer, ensuring data is clean, modeled, and ready for analysis using DBT and SQL.
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: A named transformation layer and specific DBT/Looker tooling in responsibilities indicate an existing, established data function.
evidence.jd_authorship: Hands-on experience with DBT for building, maintaining and optimizing scalable transformation workflows and data models.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Specific tool names (DBT, Looker) and a named ownership area (the transformation layer) give a concrete picture of the role.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Build, enhance and maintain dashboards using a BI tool — preferably Looker.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role centres on the transformation layer and BI dashboards serving internal data/reporting consumers rather than a specific commercial or finance function.
evidence.autonomy_level: You'll own the transformation layer, ensuring data is clean, modeled, and ready for analysis using DBT and SQL.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Ownership of the transformation layer is strategic, but the JD also frames much of the work as working closely with stakeholders to gather requirements, giving mixed autonomy.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No AI tool or AI infrastructure mention appears anywhere in the extracted JD text.
evidence.testing_framing: Ensure the accuracy, consistency and reliability of data across datasets, reports and dashboards.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Data accuracy and reliability are framed as an owned responsibility with an explicit ownership verb ("ensure"), not just a tool in a requirements list.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Strong analytical thinking, problem-solving abilities and attention to detail, with a strong focus on data accuracy, validation and reliability.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Data accuracy and validation are a recurring concern throughout, but the JD frames this as reporting quality rather than compliance or regulatory risk, keeping it moderate.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Develop, optimize and maintain scalable data models using DBT.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role mixes developing new data models with optimizing and maintaining an existing transformation layer.
evidence.loss_aversion: Strong analytical thinking, problem-solving abilities and attention to detail, with a strong focus on data accuracy, validation and reliability. — reflects concern for data reliability rather than explicit compliance/regulatory risk.
